old_claim.note: Military Field Medicine provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Roman medical supply provisioning was organized inside military hospitals and field infirmaries staffed by medici and orderlies, so military field medicine supplies the care setting.

source_shape.source_locator: The JMVH article describes Augustus-era specialization, medici tending the wounded and sick on the march and at valetudinaria, and valetudinaria developing from tents into equipped military hospitals.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Roman military medical care included medici, march care, temporary hospitals, and equipped valetudinaria.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The retained edge is not claiming that military field medicine creates every stored item; it anchors the institutional care setting where military medical supplies were stocked and used.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: state_medical_supply_stores still depends on military_field_medicine, but the edge now has a review source and a scoped note.
would_reject_if: The node is later rescoped to civilian civic stores rather than Roman military infirmary provisioning.
A source shows the earliest scoped supply practice was independent of military medical care settings.
The edge is strengthened to required without a source showing military field medicine as the only possible prerequisite.
short_reason: Roman medical supplies are anchored to military medical care settings, not an unsourced generic state-store claim.
